clean_body: NEW DOCUMENTS PROVE HASSAN AND VAN OSTERN LIED ABOUT DARTMOUTH HITCHCOCK CONTRACT Court-Ordered Release of Documents Show Hassan, Van Ostern Knew About Dartmouth Layoffs Before They Were Announced FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE: 11/5/2016 PRESS CONTACT: Ross Berry, Press@nhgop.org Concord- Today New Hampshire Republican State Committee Chair Jennifer Horn released documents ( ) that show that Governor Maggie Hassan and Councilor Colin Van Ostern lied when they said that they did not know about the recent Dartmouth-Hitchcock lay-offs before they were publicly announced by the company. The documents - released as a result of the NHGOP lawsuit against the Hassan administration - clearly show emails and a text that reference the lay-offs that were sent days before the public announcement. The timing of this lay-off announcement became an issue when Dartmouth-Hitchcock deliberately delayed it until after the Executive Council voted on its controversial $36 million contract to run New Hampshire Hospital. The lay-offs were announced on September 9, two days after the Council passed the contract on September 7. Coming under fire, both Governor Hassan and Councilor Van Ostern denied knowing about the lay-offs before they were announced by the company. The NHGOP filed a series of Right-to-Know requests for all documents related to these lay-offs. After stonewalling, the Hassan Administration finally released documents that were heavily redacted. The NHGOP retained the services of former congressman Chuck Douglas and took the Governor to court over these redactions. In response Judge Richard B. McNamara ordered the state to produce what is known as a Vaughn Index, which contains a description of the document redacted, the subject matter, and the state’s justification for the redactions. The documents produced by the Hassan Administration, as a result of this court order, clearly show that members of the Hassan administration knew about the lay-offs days before the official announcement and were discussing it amongst themselves: On September 6, 2016, there was a series of emails between Jacqueline House, Michael Power, Jeffery Rose, Carmen Lorentz, and William Hinkle with the subject line “re: D-H Layoffs”. Note that this was the day before the Council voted on the Dartmouth-Hitchcock contract and three days before the company made its official announcement. Note that William Hinkle is the governor’s spokesman, and he is later quoted as saying that the governor did not receive any advance notice of the lay-offs. Jeff Rose is the commissioner for DRED, and would be involved in any announcement of lay-offs, as would Michael Power, who heads the unit with DRED that is responsible for the rapid response effort the state launches in response to lay-offs, retraining workers and otherwise providing assistance. On September 8, 2016 at 3:45pm, there was a text from Councilor Van Ostern to Hassan chief of staff Pam Walsh with the subject “re: notice of Dartmouth-Hitchcock layoff”. This is the day after the vote, but the day before the official announcement. After Dartmouth-Hitchcock publicly announced the lay-offs, both Hassan and Van Ostern denied knowing about it beforehand: Hassan: Gov. Maggie Hassan’s office similarly learned about the planned layoffs Friday, spokesman William Hinkle said. Hassan, a Democrat now running for U.S. Senate, called it “disappointing and troubling news.” (Concord Monitor ( ), 9/10) “The governor is mobilizing the state's Rapid Response Team to provide all possible assistance to affected workers once the layoffs take place. Gov. Hassan remains disappointed by Dartmouth-Hitchcock's announcement, and the Governor's Office did not receive any information regarding the layoffs prior to the announcement,” Hinkle added. (WMUR ( ), 9/19/16) Van Ostern: QUESTION: I did want to touch on the controversy surrounding the Dartmouth-Hitchcock contract to run the state’s mental health hospital. Dartmouth-Hitchcock announcing it would lay off up to 460 employees just days after you and the other Executive Councilors voted to give them that $36 million contract. Did you have any idea that the layoffs were coming, and, if not, do you feel the council was misled? VAN OSTERN: "We were not notified and I’m disappointed to hear about the layoffs. But I also pushed very hard to make sure that this contract has specific staffing requirements, which, frankly, can’t be relaxed, regardless of the layoffs. (NHPR ( ), 9/15/16) In response to this revelation NHGOP Chair Jennifer Horn released the following statement: ‘It is absolutely stunning that Governor Hassan and Councilor Van Ostern blatantly lied to the people of New Hampshire. It has been clear from the start that they were bound and determined to deliver this $36 million contract to one of their top donors and that they would go to any length to get it done. They knew that Dartmouth-Hitchcock’s layoff announcement would torpedo the contract and they deliberately kept it from the people of New Hampshire until after the Council voted. After the news broke, they blatantly lied and said that they did not know about it beforehand. To make matters even worse, in addition to lying, Hassan has engaged in nothing short of political cover-up, delaying the release of documents and then heavily redacting them, trying to hide behind executive privilege. It took a court order to get her to produce these documents, and now we know what they were trying to hide them: these documents are the smoking gun.” “Governor Hassan’s administration has been engulfed in Clintonesque scandals and cover-ups. She has engaged in pay-to-play, rewarding her campaign donors with billions of dollars of no bid state contracts. She has been caught accepting tens of thousands of dollars of illegal campaign contributions. She lied about using taxpayer funds for her out-of-state political travel to fundraisers for her senate campaign. Her administration was even caught engaging in collusion, rigging the bidding process to ensure that her campaign donors got state contracts. And when caught she has lied, stonewalled, and tried to prevent the release of public documents. This is corruption and cover-up of the worst sort, and it is exactly what Hillary Clinton would do." Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee www.nh.gop footer image

name: Steve Bullock
subject: Hello from the road
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P
body:
---
Friend,

I've spent the past several days crisscrossing the state from Kalispell to Miles City to remind folks of the importance of getting out to vote. And I still have many miles to go before Election Day.

This is what I think this election is about -- talking with Montanans, making it clear where I stand on the issues and saying what I want to do if I earn another term. **[Will you contribute $15 for this final stretch?](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=%2BqeZ6AioOm3nEGnP4YErvKPlv5CpY7OS)**

| [](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=RKpeJDpU3RDmwDaj1i59XKPlv5CpY7OS)
---

My opponent has a different view. **He's spent almost $6 million of his own money to run the most TV ads of any gubernatorial candidate in the country.** He's refused to answer important questions and he is blatantly misrepresenting his past record on a whole range of issues, most recently claiming that he never advocated for a statewide sales tax -- even though there is clear audio of him calling it the "best solution."

Friend -- I don't think this is how we should do things here in Montana. **[This race is coming down to the wire and I need your help -- please chip in $15.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=CbjYr8f0WEP22aIVgPVvxaPlv5CpY7OS)**

Thanks,

Steve Bullock
| [**DONATE**](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=kMyITH1AXrl7Lt68OE%2F%2FMaPlv5CpY7OS)
---
| Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P.O. Box 1330, Helena, MT 59624
---
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=J4FtuunWDmkH8xIPy5%2Bf1aPlv5CpY7OS).

Friends of Steve Bullock
Post Office Box 1330
Helena MT 59624 United States
---

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: A statistical tie
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
No Senate race is more important to me -- and to the future of our democracy -- than Wisconsin. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/354577b/1130843429/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema") Friend, The polls in our battleground Senate races have me chewing my fingernails off. But nowhere am I more concerned than in Wisconsin -- where our champ Russ Feingold's lead has been clawed away to just one point. A statistical tie. The Republicans and their powerful friends believe that if they just spend an obscene amount of money in Wisconsin these last few days, they can pound Russ into the dirt and buy themselves the Senate majority. That's why they're spending $10 million to help Tea Party radical Ron Johnson between now and Election Day. **We'll never be able to match Ron Johnson's billionaire buddies dollar-for-dollar (unless you've been hiding $1 0 million under your mattress for a rainy day). But we have something they don't: YOU. And together, this grassroots team is going to prove the GOP and its Super PACs wrong by sending Russ to the Senate Tuesday night.** **[Russ needs to raise $500,000 by Election Day to fully fund his get-out- the-vote efforts. I know it's a heavy lift, but can you send $5 -- or whatever you can afford -- to help our friend Russ Feingold?](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/354577b/1130843429/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema")** [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/354577b/1130843429/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/354577b/1130843429/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [$5 »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/3545778/1130843429/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=5")
---
| | [$25 »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/3545779/1130843429/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=25")
---
| [$100 »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/3545776/1130843429/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=100")
---
| | [Other »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/3545777/1130843429/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema&express_lane_other")
---
I don't need to remind you what's at stake in this election, because the difference is clear as daylight. Ron Johnson is the Koch Brothers' "model Senator." He's one of the few Republican leaders who never wavered in his support of Donald Trump.
And when it comes to student debt, raising the minimum wage, protecting Social Security, equal pay for equal work -- you name it, and Ron Johnson consistently sides with the billionaires and giant corporations, not working families.
And what about Russ? He was our fighter long before I got to Washington. He's spent his whole career sanding up to the powerful interests -- fighting for students, for seniors, for women, for workers, for a level playing field -- and that's just what he'll do in the Senate.
That's why Senator Johnson has had the support of not one, not two, but THREE super PACs -- as well as SIXTEEN right-wing groups -- all crushing Russ with nasty, bogus attacks.
I'll be blunt: There is no path to a Democratic majority in the Senate without Wisconsin. And nobody will fight harder for the issues we care about than Russ. Everything is on the line in the next four days of this campaign.
**No Senate race is more important to me -- and to the future of our democracy -- than Wisconsin. That's why I'm asking: Will you help us raise $500, 000 to fully fund Russ's get out the vote efforts?**
**[Contribute $5 right now to help send a rock-solid progressive to fight for us in the Senate.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/354577b/1130843429/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema")**
Thanks for being a part of this, and GO RUSS!
Elizabeth
[CONTRIBUTE »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf468c/1c469400/16773bc/354577b/1130843429/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-nov?utm_source=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&refcode=ema_2016-11-05_EW_ND-rs&utm_campaign=EW&utm_medium=ema")
---

subject: losing sleep
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
Can you help us capitalize on this momentum? |

Friend, Hillary’s campaign manager said just the other day in an email to her supporters: "All that money Trump and the Republicans raised for months and didn’t seem to be spending? They're spending it now. And $140 million in advertising targeted at just a few battleground states plus a new Get-Out-The-Vote operation means I'm going to start losing sleep at night." They’re worried Friend, and that’s exactly where we want them. With your support right now, we’re going to put this race away once and for all. Step up right and donate $35, $50, or $100 to help us put this race away. Friend, we’re going to win this race. We have the momentum on our side, the polls show it, and the enormous attendance at every rally confirms it. But we cannot waste a minute. We cannot take even one vote for granted. We need to capitalize on this enthusiasm and make sure we build up a lead so large in absentee and early voting that Hillary won’t be able to win on Election Day. Can you help us capitalize on this momentum? Yes, I will help us win this election today by donating $35, $50, or $100. Friend, Hillary’s campaign has every reason to be worried and lose sleep at night. She’s covered up way too many lies, and with the FBI re-opening their investigation of her emails, the entire world is finally seeing how truly unfit she is to be our President. It’s time to make sure the rest of America knows it too. Let’s make sure they do. Donate $35, $50, or $100 right now to guarantee that Hillary and her campaign lose sleep every night until Election Day. Thank you, Eric Trump
CONTRIBUTE $100

clean_body: Can you help us capitalize on this momentum? | Friend, Hillary’s campaign manager said just the other day in an email to her supporters: "All that money Trump and the Republicans raised for months and didn’t seem to be spending? They're spending it now. And $140 million in advertising targeted at just a few battleground states plus a new Get-Out-The-Vote operation means I'm going to start losing sleep at night." They’re worried Friend, and that’s exactly where we want them. With your support right now, we’re going to put this race away once and for all. Step up right and donate $35, $50, or $100 to help us put this race away. Friend, we’re going to win this race. We have the momentum on our side, the polls show it, and the enormous attendance at every rally confirms it. But we cannot waste a minute. We cannot take even one vote for granted. We need to capitalize on this enthusiasm and make sure we build up a lead so large in absentee and early voting that Hillary won’t be able to win on Election Day. Can you help us capitalize on this momentum? Yes, I will help us win this election today by donating $35, $50, or $100. Friend, Hillary’s campaign has every reason to be worried and lose sleep at night. She’s covered up way too many lies, and with the FBI re-opening their investigation of her emails, the entire world is finally seeing how truly unfit she is to be our President. It’s time to make sure the rest of America knows it too. Let’s make sure they do. Donate $35, $50, or $100 right now to guarantee that Hillary and her campaign lose sleep every night until Election Day. Thank you, Eric Trump CONTRIBUTE $100 Contributions to the Trump Make America Great Again Committee are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. body:

| |

| Senator Burr's campaign throughout election season has been like a campfire that just won't stay ablaze.

Every few weeks over the last month, Republicans try to rekindle it by tossing a few million dollars into the heap.

**A week ago it was $3.2 million. With only three days to go before Election Day it was $1.7 million.**

Senator Burr can't get North Carolinians fired up about his campaign because he doesn't stand for much. He votes with his party, follows Trump like a puppy dog, and is the kind of leader who will watch businesses flee North Carolina without so much as a word because he doesn't want to ruffle ideological feathers.

**Thanks to your support and Deborah 's refreshing candidacy, we haven't had that problem. For more than a year, our campaign has been crackling with energy and promise.**

[_**Now we need to finish strong and bring home the win. Give $5 to help Deborah defeat Senator Burr. We 're DOUBLING donations.**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f42041/2cc31c44/43015c5/19f9c485/2966544523/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161105pm3?refcode=dre161105pm3_m&recurring=true&amount=25")

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Chris Koster Goes All In For the Clinton/Obama Agenda

Career politician Chris Koster confirmed today that he’s just another liberal, big-government Democrat politician like Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama. Koster began airing a radio ad today featuring Barack Obama.

In the ad, Obama says Koster will protect Obamacare from changes and expand it in Missouri. Obama goes on to say that even though Obama’s name isn’t on the ballot it is through Chris Koster.

The Obama ad comes after Koster’s campaign received $500,000 from Hillary Clinton and Koster took money from Planned Parenthood.

This shocking Koster ad can be heard here: (https://www.facebook.com/EricGreitens/videos/10154674188324747/?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=f4cee7a0f6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2016_11_05&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-f4cee7a0f6-99411629)

We know that the mainstream media won't cover this shocking development, so we need you to share this advertisement with everyone who cares about opposing Obamacare and the future of our state.

Justin Matheson Campaign Manager Bill Bryant for Governor (253)220-5051 | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Washingtonians for Bryant (R) PO Box 2121, Tacoma, WA 98401

name: Patrick Murphy
subject: I need you before Election Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Patrick Murphy | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida
body:
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e0e/738133e4/33b94539/7f85b8be/4065602191/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme161105pm3?refcode=pme161105pm3_m&recurring=true&amount=25")
---
| I don't have a lot of time right now. I've been busy out on the campaign trail, traveling all over Florida and earning each and every vote I can. I'm giving this my all, and I need you too.

[_**I need you to rush in a contribution.**_](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e0e/738133e4/33b94539/7f85b8be/4065602191/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme161105pm3?refcode=pme161105pm3_m&recurring=true&amount=25")

I know it's late. I know it's a weekend. But this isn't just any weekend -- it's the final weekend of the campaign.

Our field team is out knocking on doors, making phone calls, and focusing on getting our voters out on Election Day.

But if we don't have the money to continue to fund all those efforts through Tuesday, we won't defeat Marco Rubio. We won't turn the Senate blue. And we'll find out what the losing end of Election Day feels like.

I need you to dig deep. Our **final match program** of the election is in effect. [_**Every single dollar matters now -- rush in a contribution of whatever amount you can, as soon as you can, and it will be doubled.**_](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e0e/738133e4/33b94539/7f85b8be/4065602191/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme161105pm3?refcode=pme161105pm3_m&recurring=true&amount=25")

We can win this. But we all need to step it up these last few days.

Thank you for all of the support -- today and throughout this campaign.

Patrick |
Paid for by Friends of Patrick Murphy | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida.com](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2e0e/738133e4/33b94539/7f85b8bf/4065602191/VEsC/ "http://murphyforflorida.com") This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)

| **For Immediate Release: **
November 5, 2016

Contact: Ethan Latour
_ethan@philscott.org_
_802-595-3776_

**High Road to Victory Tour to Hit Top Speeds In Final Two Days**
Montpelier, Vt. – Phil Scott’s High Road to Victory Tour continues to build momentum and #riseabove it. The tour will hit its apex on Monday, November 7th at the 14th Star Brewery in St. Albans.

Joined by more than a dozen supporters, Team Scott had another productive day in Chittenden County sign waving, talking to voters and taking in local fare. “The team continues to pull ahead through our hard work and busy schedule,” said campaign spokesperson Brittney Wilson. “We’re the only campaign that can say we will be in every county in the state to talk to voters and spread our message for a more affordable Vermont before election day.” Wilson added.

The High Road to Victory Tour hits its peak on Monday, November 7th with a statewide sprint that will hit all 14 counties and conclude with a high-energy rally at 14th Star Brewing Company in St. Albans.

**Media is invited to join Phil and the team on the road. **

The schedule for Sunday, November 6th and Monday November 7th follows:

**_SUNDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2016_**

10:00 a.m. Late Breakfast/Early Lunch (Open Press)
Handy’s Lunch, 74 Maple St., Burlington

11:15 a.m. Sign Wave (Open Press)
Essex Five Corners, Essex

12:30 p.m. Sign Wave (Open Press)
Rt. 2A and Cypress St., Williston (in front of Chili’s)

2:00 – 5:00 p.m. Memorial Service (Closed)
**_MONDAY NOVEMBER 7, 2016: 14-County SPRINT:_**
4:30 - 5:00 AM Maplewood Travelers Center, Berlin
5:25 - 5:55 AM Hannaford's Shopping Place, Morrisville
6:25 - 6:55 AM Orleans, VT Park and Ride
7:10 - 7:40 AM Vermont Legion parking lot, Island Pond
8:10 - 8:40 AM Twin State Ford, St. Johnsbury
8:55 - 9:25 AM Intersection of 5 and 25B, Bradford
9:45 - 10:10 AM Bus Terminal Station, White River Jct
10:30 - 11:00 AM Nortrax, Springfield
11:25 - 11:55 AM Bond Auto Parts, Brattleboro
12:45 - 1:15 PM Tourist Center (Deer Park), Bennington
2:00 - 2:30 PM Hannafords, Rutland
3:00 - 3:30 PM Foster Motors, Middlebury
4:00 - 4:30 PM Goss Dodge, South Burlington
5:00 - 5:30 PM Apple Island General Store, South Hero
5:45 - 6:15 PM St. Albans Park, St. Albans

Following the last sign-wave we will meet up at 14th Star Brewery at 133 N Main St #7, St. Albans City to celebrate!
---
